mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate reduces equity risk premiums by confirming sustainable corporate earnings expansion, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in quarters with above-trend GDP growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating federal funds rate hikes compress equity valuations by raising the discount rate on future cash flows and signaling restrictive policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days once the 3-month acceleration threshold is breached.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rate acceleration signal, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous unemployment rate increases and payroll employment declines indicate labor market deterioration that precedes corporate revenue contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days when both series move in this adverse direction concurrently.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns more than 55% of the time over a 3-year rolling window, or the combined predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp industrial production declines during a post-shock recovery phase signal supply chain disruptions that compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days specifically when the economy is recovering from a contraction.
failure_condition: Industrial production drops do not correlate with negative equity returns during recovery periods, or the signal's predictive power disappears when GDP growth remains positive.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect sustained consumer confidence and credit availability, which historically precede broad economic expansion and support equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts show no statistically significant correlation with subsequent 5-day equity returns across multiple decades, or the signal's information coefficient remains below 0.02 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail sales volume indicates weakening consumer demand that directly threatens corporate top-line growth, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days once the quarterly decline threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: Retail sales contractions do not lead to negative equity returns, or the market prices in the decline before the 20-day forward window closes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation coupled with stagnant federal funds rates creates a negative real rate environment that distorts asset pricing and erodes purchasing power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days when both conditions persist simultaneously.
failure_condition: The combined inflation-rate signal fails to predict negative returns when real yields are positive, or the interaction effect vanishes in high-growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid unemployment deterioration during a monetary tightening cycle signals peak economic activity and impending policy-induced slowdown, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days specifically when rate hikes are active.
failure_condition: Unemployment spikes during rate hikes do not produce negative equity returns, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below chance levels in out-of-sample testing.
